Trichosanthes longicaulis, also known as Chinese snake gourd, is a plant species in the Cucurbitaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against various diseases and promote overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Trichosanthes longicaulis include cucurbitacins, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and dried extracts, which are often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.

The classification of Trichosporum longicaule is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade angiosperms, clade monocots, order Commelinales, family Commelinaceae, genus Trichosporum, species T. longicaule.
